title: Homemade Beef Burgers

overview.description: Juicy homemade beef burgers made with well-marbled mince and simply seasoned to let the beef shine.

method: Divide the mince into 4 equal portions.
Gently shape into patties approximately 2cm thick.
Press a shallow indentation into the centre of each patty with your thumb.
Season generously with the salt and pepper immediately before cooking.
Heat a heavy frying pan, griddle or barbecue until very hot.
Cook for 3–4 minutes on the first side without moving.
Cook for another 3–4 minutes for medium, adjusting to your preferred doneness.
Add the cheese during the final minute if using.
Rest the burgers for 3–5 minutes before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Many of the world's best burger restaurants season only with salt and pepper, relying on high-quality beef rather than additional ingredients.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to shape burgers correctly, avoid overworking the meat and develop a deep crust while keeping the centre juicy.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A simple family favourite that proves great burgers come from good ingredients and good technique rather than complicated recipes.
chapman-tips: Use beef with around 20% fat for the best flavour and juiciness.
Don't overwork the mince or the burgers become dense.
Season just before cooking rather than mixing salt into the meat.
Flip only once to develop a proper crust.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze uncooked patties separated with baking paper, or freeze cooked burgers once cooled.
notes.leftovers: Excellent sliced into wraps or served cold in sandwiches.
